body:
## Summary

Fixes #1296 - False positive error detection for `--tool agent`

When an agent encounters a timeout error during execution but successfully recovers and completes (indicated by `step_finish` with `reason: "stop"`), the error detection was incorrectly flagging this as a failure due to fallback pattern matching.

### Root Cause

The `agentCompletedSuccessfully` flag was only being set for `session.idle` and `"exiting loop"` log messages (Issue #1276), but not for the more common `step_finish` event with `reason: "stop"`. This meant the fallback pattern matching would still trigger and detect error patterns in the full output, even when the agent had clearly completed successfully.

### Changes

- Add `step_finish` with `reason: "stop"` as a success marker in both stdout and stderr processing loops in `src/agent.lib.mjs`
- Add test cases (Tests 24-26) in `tests/test-agent-error-detection.mjs` covering:
  - `step_finish` with reason "stop" marking successful completion
  - `step_finish` with other reasons NOT marking success
  - Full Issue #1296 scenario: timeout followed by successful completion

### Case Study

A detailed case study has been added to `docs/case-studies/issue-1296/` including:
- Timeline reconstruction from logs
- Root cause analysis
- Research on related issues and solutions
- Solution options analysis

### Test Plan

- [x] All 26 existing tests pass
- [x] New tests (24-26) specifically verify Issue #1296 fix
- [x] Lint passes

### Related Issues

- Issue #873: Phantom error detection for `--tool agent`
- Issue #886: False positive error detection for `--agent tool`
- Issue #1276: Added `agentCompletedSuccessfully` tracking
- Issue #1290: Skip fallback when agent completed successfully
- Agent Issue #183: The operation timed out (related upstream)

---

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Fix false positive error detection for step_finish with reason stop
